statements.sql: INSERT INTO silver.DimCustomer (CustomerAlternateKey, FirstName, MiddleName, LastName, Title, Gender, MaritalStatus, EmailPromotion, DateFirstPurchase) SELECT CAST(c.CustomerID AS NVARCHAR(15)) AS CustomerAlternateKey, p.FirstName, p.MiddleName, p.LastName, p.Title, NULL AS Gender, NULL AS MaritalStatus, p.EmailPromotion, CAST(h.MinOrderDate AS DATE) AS DateFirstPurchase FROM bronze.Customer c JOIN bronze.Person p ON c.PersonID = p.BusinessEntityID OUTER APPLY (SELECT MIN(OrderDate) AS MinOrderDate FROM bronze.SalesOrderHeader sh WHERE sh.CustomerID = c.CustomerID) h

refactor.extracted_sql: SELECT CAST(c.CustomerID AS NVARCHAR(15)) AS CustomerAlternateKey, p.FirstName, p.MiddleName, p.LastName, p.Title, NULL AS Gender, NULL AS MaritalStatus, p.EmailPromotion, CAST(h.MinOrderDate AS DATE) AS DateFirstPurchase FROM bronze.Customer c JOIN bronze.Person p ON c.PersonID = p.BusinessEntityID OUTER APPLY ( SELECT MIN(OrderDate) AS MinOrderDate FROM bronze.SalesOrderHeader sh WHERE sh.CustomerID = c.CustomerID ) h
refactor.refactored_sql: WITH import AS ( SELECT c.CustomerID, c.PersonID, p.FirstName, p.MiddleName, p.LastName, p.Title, p.EmailPromotion, sh.OrderDate FROM bronze.Customer c INNER JOIN bronze.Person p ON c.PersonID = p.BusinessEntityID LEFT JOIN bronze.SalesOrderHeader sh ON sh.CustomerID = c.CustomerID ), logical AS ( SELECT c.CustomerID, c.PersonID, c.FirstName, c.MiddleName, c.LastName, c.Title, c.EmailPromotion, MIN(c.OrderDate) AS MinOrderDate FROM import c GROUP BY c.CustomerID, c.PersonID, c.FirstName, c.MiddleName, c.LastName, c.Title, c.EmailPromotion ), final AS ( SELECT CAST(CustomerID AS NVARCHAR(15)) AS CustomerAlternateKey, FirstName, MiddleName, LastName, Title, NULL AS Gender, NULL AS MaritalStatus, EmailPromotion, CAST(MinOrderDate AS DATE) AS DateFirstPurchase FROM logical ) SELECT * FROM final

source_tables.summary: Both queries access bronze.Customer, bronze.Person, and bronze.SalesOrderHeader identically

output_columns.summary: Both queries produce identical output columns with matching data types and transformations (CAST, NULL literals)

joins.summary: EXTRACTED uses INNER JOIN + OUTER APPLY (scalar subquery per outer row). REFACTORED uses INNER JOIN + LEFT JOIN with explicit GROUP BY. Both semantically equivalent for the final result set: all customers are included with NULL MinOrderDate for those without orders

filters.summary: No WHERE filters in either query; both preserve all matched rows and aggregations

aggregation_grain.summary: Both produce exactly one row per customer. EXTRACTED achieves this through scalar subquery MIN() in OUTER APPLY. REFACTORED achieves this through explicit GROUP BY with MIN() aggregation. The intermediate row cardinality differs (implicit vs explicit) but the final result is identical: one row per customer with all source customers represented

issues.message: Refactored SQL uses explicit GROUP BY aggregation while extracted uses implicit scalar subquery aggregation. Both methods produce identical final results, but the refactored version is clearer and more maintainable.
